description: The core Aptitudes that define every hero in Leonoria. Physiology and Cognition are creation-fixed base aptitudes that determine resource pools and resistances. Growth Aptitudes (Martial Experience, Discipline, and formerly tracked via sub-traits) are fixed at creation and can be modified through gameplay mechanics.

mechanic_overview.fixed_aptitude_note: Physiology and Cognition do not grow through leveling — they are determined by race, class, and age at character creation. Only items, curses, afflictions, and spells can alter them after creation.

resource_pools.stamina: Derived from Physiology + age: physiology * 1.5 + ageBonus (peaks age 50, declines after 60)
resource_pools.materium: Derived from Cognition + age + race: cognition * 1.2 + ageBonus (peaks age 80) + race.materium_pool_bonus

age_effects.stamina_pool: Increases until age 50 (+15 max), then gradually declines after age 60 (to -15 at age 100)
age_effects.materium_pool: Increases until age 80 (+20 max), then flat
age_effects.hp_regeneration: Penalty increases after age 50: −0.5% base regen per year of age above 50
age_effects.affliction_susceptibility: Young (≤30): −5%. Prime (30–50): 0%. Elderly (50+): +1% per year to +30%+ at age 100

mechanic_overview.breaking_points: Certain aptitude thresholds unlock access to abilities, items, or mechanics. These are called Breaking Points.
mechanic_overview.diminishing_returns: Martial Experience gains taper with battle count — veterans learn less from standard combat than novices.
mechanic_overview.dice_connection: Conviction determines the ceiling of dice rolls. A character cannot exceed their Conviction aptitude as a maximum roll modifier on most checks.

aptitudes.name: Conviction
aptitudes.function: Controls initiative order, the ceiling of dice roll outcomes, and resistance to all mental/spiritual stressors. Also defines a character's position on the Morality axis (the core of their ethical alignment). Conviction acts as a secondary resource pool that can drain in combat but refills with successful actions.
aptitudes.flavor: The hardest thing to break is a man who has decided he will not be broken. Your convictions define who you are — and who you'll become.

sub_traits.name: Tenacity
sub_traits.description: The ability to push dice rolls to their maximum and resist immediate physical/mental shocks, fainting, and breaking under pressure.
sub_traits.leveling: Increases by surviving hardship, near-death events, and successful morale saves.

sub_traits.name: Resonance
sub_traits.description: Measures Loyalty to team and cause, and Religiosity — the depth and efficacy of faith-based actions. High Resonance creates group synergy bonuses on the battlefield. Grows with successful team outcomes and acts of faith.
sub_traits.leveling: Grows with team victories, religious actions, and consistent loyalty. Damaged by betrayal and faithlessness.

sub_sub_traits.name: Openness
sub_sub_traits.description: A fixed value set at character creation (0–10). Dictates receptivity to foreign cultures, faiths, and ideas. High Openness speeds up learning foreign Materium schools but increases vulnerability to foreign Maleficium. Also acts as the primary filter for Presence interactions — it determines whether Presence reads as Intimidating (Low) or Magnetic (High) to others.
sub_sub_traits.social_note: This value is NOT hidden. It can be perceived by experienced characters through Perception checks.

breaking_points.unlock: Can now attempt morale saves against fear effects.
Initiative bonus activates: +1 position in initiative order.
Dice ceiling raised to 90% (from 75%). All Conviction sub-traits gain +5.
Immovable Will: Once per battle, prevent a Conviction-based effect from landing.

aptitudes.name: Physiology
aptitudes.function: The biological vessel. Dictates physical limits, survival capability, and durability in all physical confrontations.
aptitudes.flavor: The body is an argument you make with the world. A strong body wins more arguments.

sub_traits.name: Vitality (HP)
sub_traits.description: The permanent health pool. Derived primarily from race, with a Physiology modifier. Cannot be easily reduced permanently — only Temporal damage and certain dark curses lower maximum Vitality.
sub_traits.leveling: Grows very slowly. Most HP gains come from race and class, not training.
sub_traits.note: Presented visually like a health bar — the primary survival indicator.

sub_traits.name: Hardening
sub_traits.description: General resistance to damage types. Multiple sub-values exist — one per damage category. Physical Hardening is trained in combat by taking hits. Magical Hardening (Insulation) levels through surviving magical attacks. Biological Hardening (Immunity) resists poisons and disease.
sub_traits.starting_range: 1–10% per type (race modifiers may increase this)
sub_traits.leveling: Slow. Each 100 hits from a type increases that resistance by ~5%.

sub_traits.name: Vigor (Strength)
sub_traits.description: Determines the ability to carry and use heavy items. Partially affects physical damage output. Negatively affects Materium affinity at very high levels (physical density chokes the flow). Increases slowly by using heavy equipment. Tapers at age 50 and then actively declines.
sub_traits.leveling: Each heavy weapon use adds a tiny increment. Physical labor also contributes.
sub_traits.conflict: High Vigor reduces Conduit Grade (Materium) by 1 for every 10 Vigor above 60.

sub_traits.name: Metabolism (Stamina)
sub_traits.description: How fast and how long a hero can move on the world map and in combat. Drains from movement, actions, and stress. Linked to Conviction — a hero can 'push' their Metabolism limit with a Conviction (Tenacity) roll at cost.
sub_traits.leveling: Increases slowly with regular active use and world map movement.

breaking_points.unlock: Can equip medium armor without class restriction.
Heavy armor available. Physical Hardening gains +3 to all types.
Vigor max increased to 80. Heavy weapons no longer cost extra Metabolism.
Stone Body: Reduce all physical damage by 10% permanently.

aptitudes.name: Cognition
aptitudes.function: The sum of learned knowledge, practical world experience, and mystical attunement. Covers scholarly learning, crafting, streetwise survival, magical capacity, and the accumulated wisdom of a life lived. Cognition determines Materium pool size, magical and curse resistances, and access to shadow schools.
aptitudes.flavor: Age is nothing. What age does is teach you what questions to ask — and some answers you can no longer live without knowing.
aptitudes.note: Creation-fixed base aptitude. Does not grow through leveling.

sub_traits.name: Erudition (Scholarly)
sub_traits.description: The ability to learn new spells, Materium theories, and complex disciplines. Higher values make learning easier — it's not just what you know, but how fast you can absorb new knowledge. Grows naturally in older heroes and through dedicated study.
sub_traits.leveling: Increases with successful spell casts, book study events, and scholarly NPCs. Grows with age regardless of use.
sub_traits.note: The Scholar class begins with significantly elevated Erudition.

sub_traits.name: Ingenuity (Crafts)
sub_traits.description: Skill in creating, repairing, and improving items from raw materials and ingredients. Applied to alchemy, smithing, magical item creation, and engineering. Trained by doing — every successful craft adds to it.

sub_traits.name: Worldliness (Common)
sub_traits.description: Practical street-level skills — lockpicking, pickpocketing, reading people, surviving in settlements, finding work. The intelligence of the street rather than the library.
sub_traits.leveling: Trained by social interactions, thievery actions, and survival events.

sub_traits.name: Scarring (Trauma)
sub_traits.description: Negative life experience that accumulates through age, battle failures, witnessing horror, and betrayal. Provides some defensive psychological grit (partial resistance to Darkmind and Verdicium at high levels) but reduces maximum Conviction as it grows. A high Scarring character has seen too much to be fully broken — or fully hopeful.
sub_traits.leveling: Increases through traumatic events. Cannot be trained down — only Soulkindler abilities can reduce it.
sub_traits.effect: Every 10 Scarring: -2 max Conviction. Above Scarring 50: +5% resistance to Darkmind and Verdicium.

sub_traits.name: Perception
sub_traits.description: The ability to detect dangers, read monster threat levels, sense traps, and notice details others miss. Levels slowly and works best in synergy with the team — a group's combined Perception averages out but suffers diminishing returns above 80% reveal chance to prevent it becoming OP.
sub_traits.leveling: Increases slowly with successful detections. Synergy note: team total Perception beyond 80% gives diminishing returns due to 'conflicting opinions' effect.

sub_traits.name: Intelligence
sub_traits.description: A fixed sub-trait set at character creation. Affects general success rate on all non-physical skill rolls. Cannot be trained — this is raw cognitive capacity, not learned skill.
sub_traits.effect: Each point adds +0.5% to all non-physical skill rolls.

sub_traits.name: Conduit Grade (Magical Channel)
sub_traits.description: The innate capacity to channel and conduct magical energy. Largely a birth-right of race and class — it can be grown very slightly through Erudition and rare items, but not meaningfully trained. Determines Materium pool size and spell power ceiling. Affected negatively by high Physiology (physical density chokes magical flow).
sub_traits.conflict: Above Physiology 60: Conduit Grade -1 per 10 Physiology. Cannot reduce below race minimum.

sub_traits.name: Shadow Connection
sub_traits.description: A unique and separate link to the Dark Arts and the Shadow Realm. Not part of the standard Materium school system — it operates through different laws. Shadow Connection powers Darkvoid, Darkblood, Chill, Darkmind, and Umbramancy. Does not interact with regular Materium pool (has its own pool sub-component).
sub_traits.starting_range: Race and class determined (0 for most non-dark races)
sub_traits.note: A character with Shadow Connection 0 cannot access Shadow Arts schools regardless of Cognition level.

sub_traits.name: Insulation (Magic Resistance)
sub_traits.description: Resistance to magical effects from all three triads. Starts at 1–10% depending on race. Levels slowly through surviving magical attacks (not by casting). Max 50%.
sub_traits.leveling: Slow. Requires surviving magical attacks to train.
sub_traits.pool_note: When Materium pool drops below 25%, Insulation effectiveness is treated as half its actual value.

breaking_points.unlock: Can access a second Materium school (if race allows).
Can now read advanced Materium texts without a Scholar in the team.
Materium pool size +15%. School mastery now possible (100 level cap in one school). Ingenuity and Erudition gain +5 to starting values for new skills in their category.
Erudition learning speed +10%.
Dual-pool: Shadow Connection and Materium pool regenerate simultaneously.
Pattern Recognition: Perception chance to detect danger raised by +10% permanently. Elemental Synergies activate at 50% bonus effectiveness instead of base.
Materium pool regenerates 2% per turn in combat.
The Sage: All skill learning rates in the Cognition tree increased by 20%. Living Conduit: Materium pool regenerates in combat at 5% per turn.

aptitudes.name: Discipline
aptitudes.function: Your initiative speed in combat. A fixed value determined by race and class at creation, randomly set within a pool. High Discipline means you act first in combat turns. Discipline is the baseline for calculating your turn order.
aptitudes.flavor: How fast do you move? How quickly do you react? That is your discipline.

mechanics.description: Discipline is a fixed aptitude that determines your base initiative value (0-100). Alignment bonuses from morality stack on top of this value.
mechanics.base_initiative: Discipline value is your base initiative in combat
mechanics.alignment_bonus: Conviction (+1 to +4) and Despair (+1 to +4) alignments add bonus to Discipline for total initiative
mechanics.neutral_no_bonus: Neutral alignment grants no additional initiative bonus

breaking_points.unlock: Base initiative 30 — standard combat speed
Base initiative 50 — faster than average combatants
Base initiative 70 — you act first in most combat encounters
Base initiative 100 — maximum action speed before modifiers
